basics.description: **Note:** Please refer to the following before installing the solution: • Review the solution [Release Notes](https://github.com/Azure/Azure-Sentinel/tree/master/Solutions/ValimailEnforce/ReleaseNotes.md) • There may be [known issues](https://aka.ms/sentinelsolutionsknownissues) pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing. The [Valimail API](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/10911206-api-overview) solution provides ability to bring the Valimail Configuration events to your Microsoft Sentinel Workspace to inform and to examine potential security risks, analyze your team's use of collaboration, diagnose configuration problems and more. **Underlying Microsoft Technologies used:** This solution takes a dependency on the following technologies, and some of these dependencies either may be in [Preview](https://azure.microsoft.com/support/legal/preview-supplemental-terms/) state or might result in additional ingestion or operational costs: a. [Azure Monitor Logs: DCR-based Custom Logs](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/logs-ingestion-api-overview) b. [Codeless Connector Framework (CCF)](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/sentinel/create-codeless-connector) **Data Connectors:** 1, **Analytic Rules:** 4, **Hunting Queries:** 4 [Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel](https://aka.ms/azuresentinel) | [Learn more about Solutions](https://aka.ms/azuresentinelsolutionsdoc)

metadata.hidden: Hiding location, we get it from the log analytics workspace

basics.name: getLAWorkspace
basics.toolTip: This filters by workspaces that exist in the Resource Group selected

basics.name: workspace
basics.label: Workspace
basics.toolTip: This dropdown will list only workspace that exists in the Resource Group selected

options.text: This Solution installs the data connector for Valimail Enforce Configuration Events. You can get Valimail Enforce Configuration Events data in your Microsoft Sentinel workspace. After installing the solution, configure and enable this data connector by following guidance in Manage solution view.

link.label: Learn more about connecting data sources

options.text: This solution installs the following analytic rule templates. After installing the solution, create and enable analytic rules in Manage solution view.

elements.name: analytic1
elements.label: Valimail Enforce - Email Authentication Key Deleted

options.text:
This query searches for deletion of SPF delegations or DKIM keys, which are medium-severity events
that could degrade email authentication posture for a domain.

elements.name: analytic2
elements.label: Valimail Enforce - DMARC Policy Weakened to None

options.text:
This query searches for DMARC policies changed to 'none', which disables enforcement
and leaves the domain vulnerable to spoofing and phishing attacks.

elements.name: analytic3
elements.label: Valimail Enforce - Unusual Rate of Configuration Changes or User Additions

options.text:
This query searches for a single user performing more than 3 configuration changes or user
additions within a 1-hour window on any domain. An unusual burst of changes may
indicate a compromised admin account, unauthorized automation, or insider threat.

elements.name: analytic4
elements.label: Valimail Enforce - High-Value User Management Event

options.text:
This query searches for high-severity user management events such as user deletion or deactivation
in Valimail Enforce, which may indicate unauthorized access or insider threat.
This solution installs the following hunting queries. After installing the solution, run these hunting queries to hunt for threats in Manage solution view.

elements.name: huntingquery1
elements.label: Valimail Enforce - Bulk Domain Changes by Single User

options.text:
Hunt for users who have made configuration changes to an unusually high number
of domains in a short period. May indicate a compromised admin account or
unauthorized bulk reconfiguration. This hunting query depends on ValimailEnforce data connector (ValimailEnforceEvents_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery2
elements.label: Valimail Enforce - Configuration Change Rate Trend

options.text:
Hunt for configuration change and user addition activity grouped by user and
domain over hourly buckets. Use this to establish baselines, spot unusual
spikes, and investigate specific users or domains flagged by the
corresponding analytics rule. This hunting query depends on ValimailEnforce data connector (ValimailEnforceEvents_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery3
elements.label: Valimail Enforce - DMARC Policy Change History

options.text:
Hunt for all DMARC policy changes across domains over the selected time range.
Helps identify domains that have had their enforcement posture changed and by whom. This hunting query depends on ValimailEnforce data connector (ValimailEnforceEvents_CL Parser or Table)

elements.name: huntingquery4
elements.label: Valimail Enforce - High Value Event Summary

options.text:
Summarizes all high-value Valimail Enforce events over the selected time range,
grouped by category and user. Good for periodic security reviews and baselining
normal admin activity. This hunting query depends on ValimailEnforce data connector (ValimailEnforceEvents_CL Parser or Table)
